paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Adults and children are frequently confronted with statements about the alarming rate of loss of tropical rainforests. For example, one graphic illustration to which children might readily relate is the estimate that rainforests are being destroyed at a rate equivalent to one thousand football fields every forty minutes - about the duration of a normal classroom period. In the face of the frequent and often vivid media coverage, it is likely that children will have formed ideas about rainforests - what and where they are, why they are important, what endangers them - independent of any formal tuition. It is also possible that some of these ideas will be mistaken.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Many studies have shown that children harbour misconceptions about "pure", curriculum science. These misconceptions do not remain isolated but become incorporated into a multifaceted, but organised, conceptual framework, making it and the component ideas, some of which are erroneous, more robust but also accessible to modification. These ideas may be developed by children absorbing ideas through the popular media. Sometimes this information may be erroneous. It seems schools may not be providing an opportunity for children to re-express their ideas and so have them tested and refined by teachers and their peers.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Despite the extensive coverage in the popular media of the destruction of rainforests, little formal information is available about children's ideas in this area. The aim of the present study is to start to provide such information, to help teachers design their educational strategies to build upon correct ideas and to displace misconceptions and to plan programmes in environmental studies in their schools.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The study surveys children's scientific knowledge and attitudes to rainforests. Secondary school children were asked to complete a questionnaire containing five open-form questions. The most frequent responses to the first question were descriptions which are self-evident from the term "rainforest". Some children described them as damp, wet or hot. The second question concerned the geographical location of rainforests. The commonest responses were continents or countries: Africa (given by 43% of children), South America (30%), Brazil (25%). Some children also gave more general locations, such as being near the Equator.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Responses to question three concerned the importance of rainforests. The dominant idea, raised by 64% of the pupils, was that rainforests provide animals with habitats. Fewer students responded that rainforests provide plant habitats, and even fewer mentioned the indigenous populations of rainforests. More girls (70%) than boys (60%) raised the idea of rainforest as animal habitats.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Similarly, but at a lower level, more girls (13%) than boys (5%) said that rainforests provided human habitats. These observations are generally consistent with our previous studies of pupils' views about the use and conservation of rainforests, in which girls were shown to be more sympathetic to animals and expressed views which seem to place an intrinsic value on non-human animal life.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The fourth question concerned the causes of the destruction of rainforests. Perhaps encouragingly, more than half of the pupils (59%) identified that it is human activities which are destroying rainforests, some personalising the responsibility by the use of terms such as "we are". About 18% of the pupils referred specifically to logging activity.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: One misconception, expressed by some 10% of the pupils, was that acid rain is responsible for rainforest destruction; a similar proportion said that pollution is destroying rainforests. Here, children are confusing rainforest destruction with damage to the forests of Western Europe by these factors. While two fifths of the students provided the information that the rainforests provide oxygen, in some cases this response also embraced the misconception that rainforest destruction would reduce atmospheric oxygen, making the atmosphere incompatible with human life on Earth.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: In answer to the final question about the importance of rainforest conservation, the majority of children simply said that we need rainforests to survive. Only a few of the pupils (6%) mentioned that rainforest destruction may contribute to global warming. This is surprising considering the high level of media coverage on this issue. Some children expressed the idea that the conservation of rainforests is not important.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: The results of this study suggest that certain ideas predominate in the thinking of children about rainforests. Pupils' responses indicate some misconceptions in basic scientific knowledge of rainforests' ecosystems such as their ideas about rainforests as habitats for animals, plants and humans and the relationship between climatic change and destruction of rainforests.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: Pupils did not volunteer ideas that suggested that they appreciated the complexity of causes of rainforest destruction. In other words, they gave no indication of an appreciation of either the range of ways in which rainforests are important or the complex social, economic and political factors which drive the activities which are destroying the rainforests. One encouragement is that the results of similar studies about other environmental issues suggest that older children seem to acquire the ability to appreciate, value and evaluate conflicting views. Environmental education offers an arena in which these skills can be developed, which is essential for these children as future decision-makers.

groups.desc:
Questions 1-8

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: The plight of the rainforests has largely been ignored by the media.
questions.analysis: 题干中的"ignored"（忽视）与原文中的"frequent"（频繁的）和"extensive"（广泛的）是反义词，表明媒体对雨林问题进行了大量报道，而不是忽视。因此，题干的说法是错误的，答案应为FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: In the face of the frequent and often vivid media coverage, it is likely that children will have formed ideas about rainforests
central_sentences.translation: 面对频繁且常常生动的媒体报道，儿童很可能已经形成了关于雨林的想法。

central_sentences.sentence: Despite the extensive coverage in the popular media of the destruction of rainforests, little formal information is available about children's ideas in this area.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管大众媒体对雨林破坏进行了广泛报道，但关于儿童在这方面的想法的正式信息很少。

target_sen.target_sen: Adults and children are frequently confronted with statements about the alarming rate of loss of tropical rainforests.

In the face of the frequent and often vivid media coverage, it is likely that children will have formed ideas about rainforests - what and where they are, why they are important, what endangers them - independent of any formal tuition.
Despite the extensive coverage in the popular media of the destruction of rainforests, little formal information is available about children's ideas in this area.

In the face of the frequent and often vivid media coverage, it is likely that children will have formed ideas about rainforests - what and where they are, why they are important, what endangers them - independent of any formal tuition.
Despite the extensive coverage in the popular media of the destruction of rainforests, little formal information is available about children's ideas in this area.

questions.title: Children only accept opinions on rainforests that they encounter in their classrooms.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“儿童只接受在课堂上遇到的关于热带雨林的观点”，而原文明确指出，儿童可能通过媒体报道形成关于热带雨林的想法，独立于任何正式教育（即课堂教学）。这与题干所述相反，因此答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: In the face of the frequent and often vivid media coverage, it is likely that children will have formed ideas about rainforests - what and where they are, why they are important, what endangers them - independent of any formal tuition.
central_sentences.translation: 面对频繁且生动的媒体报道，儿童可能已经形成了关于热带雨林的想法——它们是什么，在哪里，为什么重要，是什么威胁它们——独立于任何正式教育。

target_sen.target_sen: Adults and children are frequently confronted with statements about the alarming rate of loss of tropical rainforests.

For example, one graphic illustration to which children might readily relate is the estimate that rainforests are being destroyed at a rate equivalent to one thousand football fields every forty minutes - about the duration of a normal classroom period.

In the face of the frequent and often vivid media coverage, it is likely that children will have formed ideas about rainforests - what and where they are, why they are important, what endangers them - independent of any formal tuition.

In the face of the frequent and often vivid media coverage, it is likely that children will have formed ideas about rainforests - what and where they are, why they are important, what endangers them - independent of any formal tuition.

In the face of the frequent and often vivid media coverage, it is likely that children will have formed ideas about rainforests - what and where they are, why they are important, what endangers them - independent of any formal tuition.

questions.title: It has been suggested that children hold mistaken views about the 'pure' science that they study at school.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是TRUE，因为文章中明确提到："Many studies have shown that children harbour misconceptions about 'pure', curriculum science." 这句话直接对应了题干中的说法。"harbour misconceptions"与题干中的"hold mistaken views"意思相同，都表示"持有错误观点"。"'pure', curriculum science"对应题干中的"'pure' science that they study at school"，表示学校教授的纯粹科学。即使学生词汇量有限，只要能识别出这些关键词的对应关系，就能够正确判断答案。这道题主要考察学生对同义词的理解和辨识能力，以及对文章细节的把握。

central_sentences.sentence: Many studies have shown that children harbour misconceptions about "pure", curriculum science. These
central_sentences.translation: 许多研究表明，孩子们对“纯粹”的课程科学存在误解。

target_sen.target_sen: Many studies have shown that children harbour misconceptions about "pure", curriculum science.
These ideas may be developed by children absorbing ideas through the popular media.
It seems schools may not be providing an opportunity for children to re-express their ideas and so have them tested and refined by teachers and their peers.
Many studies have shown that children harbour misconceptions about "pure", curriculum science.

These misconceptions do not remain isolated but become incorporated into a multifaceted, but organised, conceptual framework, making it and the component ideas, some of which are erroneous, more robust but also accessible to modification.
Many studies have shown that children harbour misconceptions about "pure", curriculum science.

questions.title: The fact that children's ideas about science form part of a larger framework of ideas means that it is easier to change them.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "children's ideas about science form part of a larger framework of ideas means that it is easier to change them" 与原文中的 "These misconceptions do not remain isolated but become incorporated into a multifaceted, but organised, conceptual framework, making it and the component ideas, some of which are erroneous, more robust but also accessible to modification" 对应。原文指出这些误解被纳入一个多方面的概念框架中，使其更牢固但也更容易被修改，这与题干中的 "easier to change" 一致。因此，答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: These misconceptions do not remain isolated but become incorporated into a multifaceted, but organised, conceptual framework, making it and the component ideas, some of which are erroneous, more robust but also accessible to modification.
central_sentences.translation: 这些误解不会孤立存在，而是会被纳入一个多方面但有组织的概念框架中，使其和其中的一些错误成分变得更加牢固，但也更容易被修改。

target_sen.target_sen: Many studies have shown that children harbour misconceptions about "pure", curriculum science.

These misconceptions do not remain isolated but become incorporated into a multifaceted, but organised, conceptual framework, making it and the component ideas, some of which are erroneous, more robust but also accessible to modification.

These misconceptions do not remain isolated but become incorporated into a multifaceted, but organised, conceptual framework, making it and the component ideas, some of which are erroneous, more robust but also accessible to modification.

questions.title: The study involved asking children a number of yes/no questions such as 'Are there any rainforests in Africa?'
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词是"yes/no questions"，而原文中提到的是"open-form questions"。这两个词语的含义是相反的，开放式问题允许受访者自由回答，而是非题只能回答是或否。因此，题干中的信息与原文不符，答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Secondary school children were asked to complete a questionnaire containing five open-form questions.
central_sentences.translation: 中学生被要求完成一份包含五个开放式问题的问卷。

target_sen.target_sen: Secondary school children were asked to complete a questionnaire containing five open-form questions.

questions.title: Girls are more likely than boys to hold mistaken views about the rainforests' destruction.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“Girls”、“boys”、“mistaken views”和“rainforests' destruction”在原文中都有对应的同义替换词。然而，原文中并没有明确说明女孩比男孩更可能持有关于雨林破坏的错误观点。虽然文章提到了女孩和男孩对雨林重要性的不同看法，但没有具体比较他们在误解方面的差异。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN，因为文章没有提供足够的信息来支持或反驳这个说法。

central_sentences.sentence: More girls (70%) than boys (60%) raised the idea of rainforest as animal habitats.
central_sentences.translation: 更多的女孩（70%）比男孩（60%）提出了雨林作为动物栖息地的观点。

central_sentences.sentence: Similarly, but at a lower level, more girls (13%) than boys (5%) said that rainforests provided human habitats.
central_sentences.translation: 同样，但在较低的层次上，更多的女孩（13%）比男孩（5%）认为雨林提供了人类栖息地。

central_sentences.sentence: One misconception, expressed by some 10% of the pupils, was that acid rain is responsible for rainforest destruction; a similar proportion said that pollution is destroying rainforests.
central_sentences.translation: 大约10%的学生表达了一个误解，即酸雨是导致雨林破坏的原因；类似比例的学生认为污染正在破坏雨林。

target_sen.target_sen: More girls (70%) than boys (60%) raised the idea of rainforest as animal habitats.
Similarly, but at a lower level, more girls (13%) than boys (5%) said that rainforests provided human habitats.

These observations are generally consistent with our previous studies of pupils" views about the use and conservation of rainforests, in which girls were shown to be more sympathetic to animals and expressed views which seem to place an intrinsic value on non-human animal life.
More girls (70%) than boys (60%) raised the idea of rainforest as animal habitats.
Similarly, but at a lower level, more girls (13%) than boys (5%) said that rainforests provided human habitats.

questions.title: The study reported here follows on from a series of studies that have looked at children's understanding of rainforests.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是TRUE，因为题干中的"follows on from a series of studies"在文章中有对应的表达"Many studies have shown"，这表明本研究是基于之前的多项研究进行的。通过找到这些同义替换词，可以确定题干信息在文章中得到了验证，因此答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Many studies have shown that children harbour misconceptions about "pure", curriculum science. These
central_sentences.translation: 许多研究表明，孩子们对“纯粹”的课程科学存在误解。

target_sen.target_sen: Many studies have shown that children harbour misconceptions about "pure", curriculum science.
Many studies have shown that children harbour misconceptions about "pure", curriculum science.

questions.title: A second study has been planned to investigate primary school children's ideas about rainforests.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "second study" 和 "primary school children" 在原文中没有找到对应的同义替换词。原文只提到了一项针对中学生的研究，并没有提到计划进行第二项研究或针对小学生的研究。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到，正确答案是 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Despite the extensive coverage in the popular media of the destruction of rainforests, little formal information is available about children's ideas in this area.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管媒体广泛报道了雨林破坏的情况，但关于儿童在这方面的想法的正式信息很少。

central_sentences.sentence: The aim of the present study is to start to provide such information, to help teachers design their educational strategies to build upon correct ideas and to displace misconceptions and to plan programmes in environmental studies in their schools.
central_sentences.translation: 本研究的目的是开始提供这些信息，帮助教师设计教育策略，以建立正确的观念，消除误解，并在学校中规划环境研究课程。

The rainforests are being destroyed by the same things that are destroying the forests of Western Europe.

Without rainforests some animals would have nowhere to live.
Rainforests are important habitats for a lot of plants.
People are responsible for the loss of the rainforests.
The rainforests are a source of oxygen.

As the rainforests are destroyed, the world gets warmer.
Without rainforests there would not be enough oxygen in the air.

Rainforests are not really important to human life.
The destruction of the rainforests is the direct result of logging activity.

groups.desc:
Question 14
Choose the best answer A, B, C, D or E.

questions.title: Which of the following is the most suitable title for Reading Passage 1?

options.text: The development of a programme in environmental studies within a science curriculum
Children's ideas about the rainforests and the implications for course design
The extent to which children have been misled by the media concerning the rainforests.
How to collect, collate and describe the ideas of secondary school children.
The importance of the rainforests and the reasons for their destruction.

questions.analysis: 文章主要讨论了儿童对热带雨林的认知，以及这些认知对教育策略和课程设计的影响。因此，B选项“Children's ideas about the rainforests and the implications for course design”最为贴切。

central_sentences.sentence: The aim of the present study is to start to provide such information, to help teachers design their educational strategies to build upon correct ideas and to displace misconceptions and to plan programmes in environmental studies in their schools.
central_sentences.translation: 本研究的目的是开始提供这些信息，以帮助教师设计他们的教育策略，建立在正确的想法上，并消除误解，并在学校中规划环境研究课程。

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Some of the senses that we and other terrestrial mammals take for granted are either reduced or absent in cetaceans or fail to function well in water. For example, it appears from their brain structure that toothed species are unable to smell. Baleen species, on the other hand, appear to have some related brain structures but it is not known whether these are functional. It has been speculated that, as the blowholes evolved and migrated to the top of the head, the neural pathways serving sense of smell may have been nearly all sacrificed. Similarly, although at least some cetaceans have taste buds, the nerves serving these have degenerated or are rudimentary.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The sense of touch has sometimes been described as weak too, but this view is probably mistaken. Trainers of captive dolphins and small whales often remark on their animals' responsiveness to being touched or rubbed, and both captive and free-ranging cetacean individuals of all species (particularly adults and calves, or members of the same subgroup) appear to make frequent contact. This contact may help to maintain order within a group, and stroking or touching are part of the courtship ritual in most species. The area around the blowhole is also particularly sensitive and captive animals often object strongly to being touched there.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The sense of vision is developed to different degrees in different species. Baleen species studied at close quarters underwater - specifically a grey whale calf in captivity for a year, and free-ranging right whales and humpback whales studied and filmed off Argentina and Hawaii - have obviously tracked objects with vision underwater, and they can apparently see moderately well both in water and in air. However, the position of the eyes so restricts the field of vision in baleen whales that they probably do not have stereoscopic vision.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: On the other hand, the position of the eyes in most dolphins and porpoises suggests that they have stereoscopic vision forward and downward. Eye position in freshwater dolphins, which often swim on their side or upside down while feeding, suggests that what vision they have is stereoscopic forward and upward. By comparison, the bottlenose dolphin has extremely keen vision in water. Judging from the way it watches and tracks airborne flying fish, it can apparently see fairly well through the air-water interface as well. And although preliminary experimental evidence suggests that their in-air vision is poor, the accuracy with which dolphins leap high to take small fish out of a trainer's hand provides anecdotal evidence to the contrary.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Such variation can no doubt be explained with reference to the habitats in which individual species have developed. For example, vision is obviously more useful to species inhabiting clear open waters than to those living in turbid rivers and flooded plains. The South American boutu and Chinese beiji, for instance, appear to have very limited vision, and the Indian susus are blind, their eyes reduced to slits that probably allow them to sense only the direction and intensity of light.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text:
Although the senses of taste and smell appear to have deteriorated, and vision in water appears to be uncertain, such weaknesses are more than compensated for by cetaceans' well-developed acoustic sense. Most species are highly vocal, although they vary in the range of sounds they produce, and many forage for food using echolocation①. Large baleen whales primarily use the lower frequencies and are often limited in their repertoire. Notable exceptions are the nearly song-like choruses of bowhead whales in summer and the complex, haunting utterances of the humpback whales. Toothed species in general employ more of the frequency spectrum, and produce a wider variety of sounds, than baleen species (though the sperm whale apparently produces a monotonous series of high-energy clicks and little else). Some of the more complicated sounds are clearly communicative, although what role they may play in the social life and "culture" of cetaceans has been more the subject of wild speculation than of solid science.
① Echolocation: the perception of objects by means of sound wave echoes.

probably only sense direction and intensity of light
use more of frequency spectrum; have wider repertoire

questions.analysis: 正确答案是"taste buds"，因为文章中提到："although at least some cetaceans have taste buds, the nerves serving these have degenerated or are rudimentary." 这句话的意思是：尽管至少一些鲸类有味蕾，但是支配这些味蕾的神经已经退化或者是原始的（未发育的）。"taste buds"（味蕾）正是与味觉相关的器官，而题目中要求填写的是与味觉（taste）相关的内容。文章明确指出，虽然鲸类有味蕾，但支配这些味蕾的神经是退化或未发育的，这正好对应了题目中"nerves linked to their [...] are underdeveloped"（与它们的[...]相连的神经未发育）这一描述。因此，"taste buds"是最合适的答案，它既符合题目要求的与味觉相关，又与文章中描述的未发育神经直接相关。

central_sentences.sentence: although at least some cetaceans have taste buds, the nerves serving these have degenerated or are rudimentary.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管至少一些鲸类有味蕾，但支配这些味蕾的神经已经退化或未发育。

target_sen.target_sen: Similarly, although at least some cetaceans have taste buds, the nerves serving these have degenerated or are rudimentary.
Similarly, although at least some cetaceans have taste buds, the nerves serving these have degenerated or are rudimentary.
Similarly, although at least some cetaceans have taste buds, the nerves serving these have degenerated or are rudimentary.
Similarly, although at least some cetaceans have taste buds, the nerves serving these have degenerated or are rudimentary.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 "baleen whales" 的原因是文章第三段专门讨论了须鲸 (baleen whales) 的视力情况。关键句是 "Baleen species studied at close quarters underwater ... have obviously tracked objects with vision underwater, and they can apparently see moderately well both in water and in air." 这句话明确指出须鲸可以在水下和空气中看到东西，虽然视力不是很好。因此，"baleen whales" 是最符合题目要求的答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The sense of vision is developed to different degrees in different species.
central_sentences.translation: 不同物种的视力发育程度不同。

central_sentences.sentence: Baleen species studied at close quarters underwater - specifically a grey whale calf in captivity for a year, and free-ranging right whales and humpback whales studied and filmed off Argentina and Hawaii - have obviously tracked objects with vision underwater, and they can apparently see moderately well both in water and in air.
central_sentences.translation: 在水下近距离研究的须鲸物种——特别是一只被圈养了一年的灰鲸幼崽，以及在阿根廷和夏威夷研究和拍摄的自由活动的露脊鲸和座头鲸——显然能够用视力在水下追踪物体，并且它们在水中和空气中都能看得相当清楚。

target_sen.target_sen: The sense of vision is developed to different degrees in different species.

Baleen species studied at close quarters underwater - specifically a grey whale calf in captivity for a year, and free-ranging right whales and humpback whales studied and filmed off Argentina and Hawaii - have obviously tracked objects with vision underwater, and they can apparently see moderately well both in water and in air.
However, the position of the eyes so restricts the field of vision in baleen whales that they probably do not have stereoscopic vision.
The sense of vision is developed to different degrees in different species.

Baleen species studied at close quarters underwater - specifically a grey whale calf in captivity for a year, and free-ranging right whales and humpback whales studied and filmed off Argentina and Hawaii - have obviously tracked objects with vision underwater, and they can apparently see moderately well both in water and in air.
However, the position of the eyes so restricts the field of vision in baleen whales that they probably do not have stereoscopic vision.
The sense of vision is developed to different degrees in different species.

Baleen species studied at close quarters underwater - specifically a grey whale calf in captivity for a year, and free-ranging right whales and humpback whales studied and filmed off Argentina and Hawaii - have obviously tracked objects with vision underwater, and they can apparently see moderately well both in water and in air.
The sense of vision is developed to different degrees in different species.

Baleen species studied at close quarters underwater - specifically a grey whale calf in captivity for a year, and free-ranging right whales and humpback whales studied and filmed off Argentina and Hawaii - have obviously tracked objects with vision underwater, and they can apparently see moderately well both in water and in air.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 "forward and downward"。题干中的 "probably have stereoscopic vision" 与原文中的 "suggests that they have stereoscopic vision" 是同义替换。原文句子明确指出大多数海豚和鼠海豚的眼睛位置表明它们具有向前和向下的立体视觉，因此答案是 "forward and downward"。

central_sentences.sentence: On the other hand, the position of the eyes in most dolphins and porpoises suggests that they have stereoscopic vision forward and downward.
central_sentences.translation: 另一方面，大多数海豚和鼠海豚的眼睛位置表明它们可能具有向前和向下的立体视觉。

syn_ary.synonym: suggests that they have stereoscopic vision

target_sen.target_sen: On the other hand, the position of the eyes in most dolphins and porpoises suggests that they have stereoscopic vision forward and downward.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是"freshwater dolphins"，因为文章中明确提到："Eye position in freshwater dolphins, which often swim on their side or upside down while feeding, suggests that what vision they have is stereoscopic forward and upward." 这句话直接描述了淡水海豚的眼睛位置和视力特点，与题目要求的"Vision"和"Species"完全对应。这个描述是独特的，只适用于淡水海豚这个特定物种，而不是其他提到的海洋生物。

central_sentences.sentence: Eye position in freshwater dolphins, which often swim on their side or upside down while feeding, suggests that what vision they have is stereoscopic forward and upward.
central_sentences.translation: 淡水海豚的眼睛位置表明，它们在觅食时经常侧身或倒立游泳，这表明它们的视力是向前和向上的立体视觉。

target_sen.target_sen: Eye position in freshwater dolphins, which often swim on their side or upside down while feeding, suggests that what vision they have is stereoscopic forward and upward.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 "water"，因为文章明确指出："the bottlenose dolphin has extremely keen vision in water"。这句话直接对应了题干中的 "exceptional in [ ]"。虽然文章也提到海豚能够通过空气-水界面看得相当好，但题目要求填写的是海豚视力特别出色的环境，那就是水中。

central_sentences.sentence: the bottlenose dolphin has extremely keen vision in water
central_sentences.translation: 宽吻海豚在水中的视力非常敏锐。

central_sentences.sentence: it can apparently see fairly well through the air-water interface
central_sentences.translation: 它显然可以通过空气-水界面看得相当好。

target_sen.target_sen: By comparison, the bottlenose dolphin has extremely keen vision in water.
By comparison, the bottlenose dolphin has extremely keen vision in water.

syn_ary.synonym: see fairly well through the air-water interface

target_sen.target_sen: Judging from the way it watches and tracks airborne flying fish, it can apparently see fairly well through the air-water interface as well.
Judging from the way it watches and tracks airborne flying fish, it can apparently see fairly well through the air-water interface as well.

questions.analysis: 题目中的线索词是“repertoire limited”，在原文中找到了对应的同义替换词“limited in their repertoire”。原文句子明确指出大型须鲸主要使用低频声音（lower frequencies），并且它们的声音范围是有限的。因此，答案是“lower frequencies”。

central_sentences.sentence: Large baleen whales primarily use the lower frequencies and are often limited in their repertoire.
central_sentences.translation: 大型须鲸主要使用低频声音，并且它们的声音范围通常是有限的。

target_sen.target_sen: Large baleen whales primarily use the lower frequencies and are often limited in their repertoire.

questions.analysis: 题目中的"Hearing"对应文章中的"acoustic sense"，而"Species"对应文章中的"bowhead whales"和"humpback whales"。文章中明确提到弓头鲸和座头鲸发出独特的声音，因此答案是"bowhead and humpback"。

central_sentences.sentence: Notable exceptions are the nearly song-like choruses of bowhead whales in summer and the complex, haunting utterances of the humpback whales.
central_sentences.translation: 值得注意的例外是弓头鲸在夏天发出的类似歌声的合唱，以及座头鲸发出的复杂、令人难忘的声音。

target_sen.target_sen: Although the senses of taste and smell appear to have deteriorated, and vision in water appears to be uncertain, such weaknesses are more than compensated for by cetaceans" well-developed acoustic sense.
Notable exceptions are the nearly song-like choruses of bowhead whales in summer and the complex, haunting utterances of the humpback whales.
Notable exceptions are the nearly song-like choruses of bowhead whales in summer and the complex, haunting utterances of the humpback whales.

groups.desc:
Questions 22-26

Answer the questions below using NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS from the passage for each answer.

questions.title: Which of the senses is described here as being involved in mating?
questions.analysis: 题干中的"mating"对应原文中的"courtship ritual"，而"senses"对应原文中的"sense of touch"。原文明确提到"stroking or touching are part of the courtship ritual"，说明触觉在求偶仪式中起作用。因此，正确答案是"sense of touch"。

central_sentences.sentence: stroking or touching are part of the courtship ritual in most species.
central_sentences.translation: 在大多数物种中，抚摸或触摸是求偶仪式的一部分。

target_sen.target_sen: This contact may help to maintain order within a group, and stroking or touching are part of the courtship ritual in most species.
The sense of touch has sometimes been described as weak too, but this view is probably mistaken.

questions.title: Which species swims upside down while eating?
questions.analysis: 题干中的"swims upside down while eating"与原文中的"swim on their side or upside down while feeding"直接对应。通过定位到这句话，可以确定答案是"freshwater dolphins"。即使词汇量有限，学生也可以通过搜索关键词"upside down"来找到相关句子，并从句子开头找出表示物种的词汇。其他选项如"bottlenose dolphin"和"baleen whales"虽然在文章中提到，但没有提到它们倒立游泳，因此不正确。

central_sentences.sentence: Eye position in freshwater dolphins, which often swim on their side or upside down while feeding, suggests that what vision they have is stereoscopic forward and upward.
central_sentences.translation: 淡水海豚的眼睛位置表明，它们在进食时经常侧身或倒立游泳，这表明它们的视力是向前和向上的立体视力。

syn_ary.synonym: swim on their side or upside down

target_sen.target_sen: Eye position in freshwater dolphins, which often swim on their side or upside down while feeding, suggests that what vision they have is stereoscopic forward and upward.
Eye position in freshwater dolphins, which often swim on their side or upside down while feeding, suggests that what vision they have is stereoscopic forward and upward.

questions.title: What can bottlenose dolphins follow from under the water?
questions.analysis: 题干中的"bottlenose dolphins"对应原文中的"bottlenose dolphin"，"follow"对应原文中的"watches and tracks"，"from under the water"对应原文中的"through the air-water interface"。通过这些对应关系，可以定位到原文中的句子"Judging from the way it watches and tracks airborne flying fish, it can apparently see fairly well through the air-water interface as well."。从这句话中可以看出，bottlenose dolphins可以从水下追踪"airborne flying fish"，因此答案是"airborne flying fish"。

central_sentences.sentence: Judging from the way it watches and tracks airborne flying fish, it can apparently see fairly well through the air-water interface as well.
central_sentences.translation: 从它观察和追踪空中飞鱼的方式来看，它显然也能很好地通过空气-水界面看到东西。

target_sen.target_sen: By comparison, the bottlenose dolphin has extremely keen vision in water.
Judging from the way it watches and tracks airborne flying fish, it can apparently see fairly well through the air-water interface as well.
Judging from the way it watches and tracks airborne flying fish, it can apparently see fairly well through the air-water interface as well.

questions.title: Which type of habitat is related to good visual ability?
questions.answer: clear waters / clear open waters / open waters
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词是“habitat”和“visual ability”，在原文中分别对应“habitats”和“vision”。通过定位到包含这些词的句子，我们找到“vision is obviously more useful to species inhabiting clear open waters”这句话。这里明确指出了“clear open waters”与良好的视力能力相关。因此，答案是“clear waters”。

central_sentences.sentence: vision is obviously more useful to species inhabiting clear open waters
central_sentences.translation: 视力显然对栖息在清澈开阔水域的物种更有用。

target_sen.target_sen: Such variation can no doubt be explained with reference to the habitats in which individual species have developed.
For example, vision is obviously more useful to species inhabiting clear open waters than to those living in turbid rivers and flooded plains.

The South American boutu and Chinese beiji, for instance, appear to have very limited vision, and the Indian susus are blind, their eyes reduced to slits that probably allow them to sense only the direction and intensity of light.

questions.title: Which of the senses is best developed in cetaceans?
questions.analysis: 题干中的"best developed"对应原文中的"well-developed"，而"senses"对应原文中的"sense"。原文明确指出鲸类的听觉(acoustic sense)是发达的，且弥补了其他感官的不足。因此，答案是"acoustic sense"。

central_sentences.sentence: Although the senses of taste and smell appear to have deteriorated, and vision in water appears to be uncertain, such weaknesses are more than compensated for by cetaceans" well-developed acoustic sense. Most
central_sentences.translation: 尽管味觉和嗅觉似乎已经退化，水中的视力也不确定，但这些弱点被鲸类发达的听觉所弥补。

target_sen.target_sen: Although the senses of taste and smell appear to have deteriorated, and vision in water appears to be uncertain, such weaknesses are more than compensated for by cetaceans" well-developed acoustic sense.

Although the senses of taste and smell appear to have deteriorated, and vision in water appears to be uncertain, such weaknesses are more than compensated for by cetaceans" well-developed acoustic sense.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Part 1

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: From a number of recent studies, it has become clear that blind people can appreciate the use of outlines and perspectives to describe the arrangement of objects and other surfaces in space. But pictures are more than literal representations. This fact was drawn to my attention dramatically when a blind woman in one of my investigations decided on her own initiative to draw a wheel as it was spinning. To show this motion, she traced a curve inside the circle (Fig. 1). I was taken aback. Lines of motion, such as the one she used, are a very recent invention in the history of illustration. Indeed, as art scholar David Kunzle notes, Wilhelm Busch, a trend-setting nineteenth-century cartoonist, used virtually no motion lines in his popular figures until about 1877.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: When I asked several other blind study subjects to draw a spinning wheel, one particularly clever rendition appeared repeatedly: several subjects showed the wheel's spokes as curved lines. When asked about these curves, they all described them as metaphorical ways of suggesting motion. Majority rule would argue that this device somehow indicated motion very well. But was it a better indicator than, say, broken or wavy lines – or any other kind of line, for that matter? The answer was not clear. So I decided to test whether various lines of motion were apt ways of showing movement or if they were merely idiosyncratic marks. Moreover, I wanted to discover whether there were differences in how the blind and the sighted interpreted lines of motion.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: To search out these answers, I created raised-line drawings of five different wheels, depicting spokes with lines that curved, bent, waved, dashed and extended beyond the perimeter of the wheel. I then asked eighteen blind volunteers to feel the wheels and assign one of the following motions to each wheel: wobbling, spinning fast, spinning steadily, jerking or braking. My control group consisted of eighteen sighted undergraduates from the University of Toronto.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: All but one of the blind subjects assigned distinctive motions to each wheel. Most guessed that the curved spokes indicated that the wheel was spinning steadily; the wavy spokes, they thought, suggested that the wheel was wobbling; and the bent spokes were taken as a sign that the wheel was jerking. Subjects assumed that spokes extending beyond the wheel's perimeter signified that the wheel had its brakes on and that dashed spokes indicated the wheel was spinning quickly.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: In addition, the favoured description for the sighted was the favoured description for the blind in every instance. What is more, the consensus among the sighted was barely higher than that among the blind. Because motion devices are unfamiliar to the blind, the task I gave them involved some problem solving. Evidently, however, the blind not only figured out meanings for each line of motion, but as a group they generally came up with the same meaning at least as frequently as did sighted subjects.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Part 2

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: We have found that the blind understand other kinds of visual metaphors as well. One blind woman drew a picture of a child inside a heart – choosing that symbol, she said, to show that love surrounded the child. With Chang Hong Liu, a doctoral student from China, I have begun exploring how well blind people understand the symbolism behind shapes such as hearts that do not directly represent their meaning.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: We gave a list of twenty pairs of words to sighted subjects and asked them to pick from each pair the term that best related to a circle and the term that best related to a square. For example, we asked: What goes with soft? A circle or a square? Which shape goes with hard?

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: All our subjects deemed the circle soft and the square hard. A full 94% ascribed happy to the circle, instead of sad. But other pairs revealed less agreement: 79% matched fast to slow and weak to strong, respectively. And only 51% linked deep to circle and shallow to square. (See Fig. 2.) When we tested four totally blind volunteers using the same list, we found that their choices closely resembled those made by the sighted subjects. One man, who had been blind since birth, scored extremely well. He made only one match differing from the consensus, assigning "far" to square and "near" to circle. In fact, only a small majority of sighted subjects – 53% – had paired far and near to the opposite partners. Thus, we concluded that the blind interpret abstract shapes as sighted people do.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-29

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write your answers in boxes 27-29 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: In the first paragraph the writer makes the point that blind people

options.text: may be interested in studying art.
can draw outlines of different objects and surfaces.
can recognise conventions such as perspective.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C，因为文章第一句话明确提到盲人能够理解使用轮廓和透视法（appreciate the use of outlines and perspectives），这与选项C中的“recognise conventions such as perspective”是同义表达。题干问的是盲人能做什么，文章给出的答案是他们能理解透视法等绘画惯例。

central_sentences.sentence: From a number of recent studies, it has become clear that blind people can appreciate the use of outlines and perspectives to describe the arrangement of objects and other surfaces in space.
central_sentences.translation: 从一些最近的研究中可以清楚地看出，盲人能够理解使用轮廓和透视法来描述物体和其他表面在空间中的排列。

questions.title: The writer was surprised because the blind woman

options.text: drew a circle on her own initiative.
did not understand what a wheel looked like.
was the first person to use lines of motion.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为文章中提到盲人女性为了展示运动，在圆圈内描绘了一条曲线，这是一种表示运动的符号。作者对此感到惊讶。

central_sentences.sentence: To show this motion, she traced a curve inside the circle (Fig. 1).
central_sentences.translation: 为了展示这个运动，她在圆圈内描绘了一条曲线（图1）。

questions.title: From the experiment described in Part 1, the writer found that the blind subjects

options.text: had good understanding of symbols representing movement.
could control the movement of wheels very accurately.
worked together well as a group in solving problems.
got better results than the sighted undergraduates.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A，因为文章明确指出盲人理解了表示运动的符号（lines of motion）。文章中的关键句表明盲人能够理解这些符号的含义，并且他们的理解与明眼人相似。这与选项A的意思完全吻合。

central_sentences.sentence: Evidently, however, the blind not only figured out meanings for each line of motion, but as a group they generally came up with the same meaning at least as frequently as did sighted subjects.
central_sentences.translation: 显然，盲人不仅理解了每条运动线的含义，而且作为一个群体，他们得出的结论与明眼人一样频繁。

groups.desc:
Questions 30-32
Look at the following diagrams (Questions 30-32), and the list of types of movement below.
Match each diagram to the type of movement A-E generally assigned to it in the experiment.
Choose the correct letter A-E and write them in boxes 30-32 on your answer sheet.

questions.analysis: 第30题的图形特征是辐条延伸出了轮子的边界。文章中明确指出，辐条延伸出轮子边界表示轮子在刹车。因此，正确答案是E（use of brakes）。

central_sentences.sentence: Subjects assumed that spokes extending beyond the wheel's perimeter signified that the wheel had its brakes on
central_sentences.translation: 受试者认为，辐条延伸出轮子边界表示轮子在刹车。

target_sen.target_sen: Subjects assumed that spokes extending beyond the wheel's perimeter signified that the wheel had its brakes on and that dashed spokes indicated the wheel was spinning quickly.

questions.analysis: 第31题的图示显示的是一个带有虚线辐条的轮子。根据原文，虚线辐条（dashed spokes）表示快速旋转（spinning quickly），因此正确答案是C。通过识别图示特征和文章中的描述，可以准确找到对应的答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Subjects assumed that spokes extending beyond the wheel's perimeter signified that the wheel had its brakes on and that dashed spokes indicated the wheel was spinning quickly.
central_sentences.translation: 受试者认为，超出车轮周边的辐条表示车轮正在刹车，而虚线辐条表示车轮快速旋转。

questions.analysis: 第32题的图中显示的是弯曲的辐条（curved spokes），文章中明确指出弯曲的辐条表示“spinning steadily”，即“steady spinning”。因此，正确答案是A。

central_sentences.sentence: Most guessed that the curved spokes indicated that the wheel was spinning steadily; the
central_sentences.translation: 大多数人猜测，弯曲的辐条表示车轮在平稳旋转。

target_sen.target_sen: Most guessed that the curved spokes indicated that the wheel was spinning steadily; the wavy spokes, they thought, suggested that the wheel was wobbling; and the bent spokes were taken as a sign that the wheel was jerking.

Most guessed that the curved spokes indicated that the wheel was spinning steadily; the wavy spokes, they thought, suggested that the wheel was wobbling; and the bent spokes were taken as a sign that the wheel was jerking.

groups.desc:
Questions 33-39

Complete the summary below using words from the box.
Write your answers in boxes 33-39 on your answer sheet.
NB You may use any word more than once.
groups.collect: In the experiment described in Part 2, a set of word $34329$ was used to investigate whether blind and sighted people perceived the symbolism in abstract $34330$ in the same way. Subjects were asked which word fitted best with a circle and which with a square. From the $34331$ volunteers, everyone thought a circle fitted 'soft' while a square fitted 'hard'. However, only 51% of the $34332$ volunteers assigned a circle to $34333$ When the test was later repeated with $34334$ volunteers, it was found that they made $34335$ choices. OPTIONS: associations    blind    deep    hard    handred    identical    pairs    shapes    sighted    similar    shallow    soft    words

questions.analysis: 答案是"pairs"。题干中的"word 【 】"对应文章中的"twenty pairs of words"。文章明确提到实验中使用了"twenty pairs of words"，这与题干中的描述完全一致。因此，正确答案是"pairs"。

central_sentences.sentence: We gave a list of twenty pairs of words to sighted subjects and asked them to pick from each pair the term that best related to a circle and the term that best related to a square.
central_sentences.translation: 我们给明眼人提供了一份包含二十对词语的清单，并要求他们从每对词语中选择一个最能与圆形相关的词语和一个最能与方形相关的词语。

target_sen.target_sen: We gave a list of twenty pairs of words to sighted subjects and asked them to pick from each pair the term that best related to a circle and the term that best related to a square.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "abstract" 和 "shapes" 在原文中直接对应 "abstract shapes"。原文最后一句话明确指出盲人和明眼人对抽象形状的理解相同，这与题干的描述完全一致。因此，答案是 "shapes"。

central_sentences.sentence: Thus, we concluded that the blind interpret abstract shapes as sighted people do.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，我们得出结论，盲人对抽象形状的理解与明眼人相同。

target_sen.target_sen: Thus, we concluded that the blind interpret abstract shapes as sighted people do.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“volunteers”在原文中对应的是“subjects”，而“circle fitted 'soft'”和“square fitted 'hard'”在原文中分别对应“circle soft”和“square hard”。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定原文中提到的“sighted subjects”就是题干中提到的“volunteers”，因此答案是“sighted”。

central_sentences.sentence: We gave a list of twenty pairs of words to sighted subjects and asked them to pick from each pair the term that best related to a circle and the term that best related to a square. For example, we asked: What goes with soft? A circle or a square? Which shape goes with hard
central_sentences.translation: 我们给视力正常的受试者提供了一份包含二十对词语的列表，并要求他们从每对词语中选择一个最适合圆形的词语和一个最适合方形的词语。例如，我们问：什么与“软”相配？圆形还是方形？哪个形状与“硬”相配？所有受试者都认为圆形代表“软”，方形代表“硬”。

target_sen.target_sen: We gave a list of twenty pairs of words to sighted subjects and asked them to pick from each pair the term that best related to a circle and the term that best related to a square.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“51% of the [...] volunteers”对应原文中的“only 51% linked deep to circle and shallow to square”。这里的51%指的是有视力的受试者（sighted subjects）。因此，题干中的空格应填入“sighted”，表示51%的有视力志愿者将圆形与“deep”联系起来。这样既符合文章内容，也符合题干后半句提到随后对盲人志愿者进行了测试这一逻辑顺序。

central_sentences.sentence: only 51% linked deep to circle and shallow to square.
central_sentences.translation: 只有51%的人将“深”与圆形联系起来，将“浅”与方形联系起来。

target_sen.target_sen: And only 51% linked deep to circle and shallow to square.
All our subjects deemed the circle soft and the square hard.
When we tested four totally blind volunteers using the same list, we found that their choices closely resembled those made by the sighted subjects.
In fact, only a small majority of sighted subjects - 53% - had paired far and near to the opposite partners.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“51% of the volunteers assigned a circle to”对应原文中的“only 51% linked deep to circle”。通过这个对应关系，我们可以确定答案是“deep”。题目要求填入的是与“circle”相关的词，而原文明确指出“51%的人将"深"与圆形联系在一起”，因此答案是“deep”。

central_sentences.sentence: And only 51% linked deep to circle and shallow to square.
central_sentences.translation: 只有51%的人将“深”与圆形联系在一起，将“浅”与方形联系在一起。

target_sen.target_sen: And only 51% linked deep to circle and shallow to square.
And only 51% linked deep to circle and shallow to square.

questions.analysis: 题干中的"volunteers"和"repeated"在原文中分别对应"volunteers"和"tested"。原文中提到"four totally blind volunteers"，并且他们的选择与视力正常的受试者非常相似。因此，答案是"blind"。这个答案正确是因为题干要求填入描述志愿者类型的词，而原文明确指出这些志愿者是"blind"。

central_sentences.sentence: When we tested four totally blind volunteers using the same list, we found that their choices closely resembled those made by the sighted subjects.
central_sentences.translation: 当我们用同样的列表测试四名完全失明的志愿者时，我们发现他们的选择与视力正常的受试者的选择非常相似。

target_sen.target_sen: When we tested four totally blind volunteers using the same list, we found that their choices closely resembled those made by the sighted subjects.
When we tested four totally blind volunteers using the same list, we found that their choices closely resembled those made by the sighted subjects.
When we tested four totally blind volunteers using the same list, we found that their choices closely resembled those made by the sighted subjects.

questions.analysis: 答案是“similar”，因为文章中提到“their choices closely resembled those made by the sighted subjects”，意思是盲人志愿者的选择与有视力的受试者的选择非常相似。题干中的“volunteers”和“choices”在原文中也有直接对应的词语，因此可以确定答案。

central_sentences.sentence: we found that their choices closely resembled those made by the sighted subjects. One
central_sentences.translation: 我们发现他们的选择与有视力的受试者的选择非常相似。

target_sen.target_sen: When we tested four totally blind volunteers using the same list, we found that their choices closely resembled those made by the sighted subjects.
When we tested four totally blind volunteers using the same list, we found that their choices closely resembled those made by the sighted subjects.

groups.desc:
Question 40

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write your answer in box 40 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: Which of the following statements best summarises the writer's general conclusion?

options.text: The blind represent some aspects of reality differently from sighted people.
The blind comprehend visual metaphors in similar ways to sighted people.
The blind may create unusual and effective symbols to represent reality.
The blind may be successful artists if given the right training.

questions.analysis: 文章最后一句话明确指出，盲人对抽象形状的解释与有视力的人相同，这与选项B的表述完全一致。因此，选项B是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Thus, we concluded that the blind interpret abstract shapes as sighted people do.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，我们得出结论，盲人对抽象形状的解释与有视力的人相同。
